title: Alicha Wot with Corn Fritters
description: A traditional Ethiopian dish, Alicha Wot is a mild and flavorful stew made with spices, onions, and a choice of protein. Paired with crispy corn fritters, this meal is a delicious and satisfying option.

ingredients.name: Rousing
ingredients.notes: Water used for soaking and cooking

instructions: Soak the kik (lentils) in the rousing (water) for 30 minutes. Drain and set aside.
Heat oil in a pan over medium heat. Add the onion and cook until softened.
Add the Berbere spice mix (Ethiopian) and cook for 1 minute.
Add the kik (lentils) and 2 cups of rousing (water). Bring to a boil, then reduce heat and simmer.
Cook the corn on the cob by boiling in water until tender. Set aside.
In a separate bowl, mix together the flour, a pinch of salt, and a dash of water to make the fritter batter.
Heat oil in a pan over medium-high heat. Drop spoonfuls of the fritter batter into the oil and fry until crispy and golden.
Serve the Alicha Wot with the corn fritters on the side.
